title: --group-add option does not affect?
body:
### Issue Description

we want to shared a  volume   between rootless containers,  we try it  and  reference to https://www.redhat.com/en/blog/supplemental-groups-podman-containers , however it always throw ```permission denied```

podman version is:
```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.5.2
API Version:  5.5.2
Go Version:   go1.24.4
Git Commit:   e7d8226745ba07a64b7176a7f128e4ef53225a0e
Built:        Wed Jun 25 11:45:49 2025
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

os version is:
```
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION_ID="24.04"
VERSION="24.04.2 LTS (Noble Numbat)"
VERSION_CODENAME=noble
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy"
UBUNTU_CODENAME=noble
LOGO=ubuntu-logo
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
```
1. mkdir /data/shared
2. groupadd sharedgroup
3. chown -R root:sharedgroup /data/shared
4. chmod -R 770 /data/shared
5. adduser user03
6. adduser user04
7. usermod -aG sharedgroup user03
8. usermod -aG sharedgroup user04
9. sudo -u user03 podman run -it -d  -v /data/shared:/shared --network=none --name user03 --userns=keep-id --group-add keep-groups --log-level debug quay.io/travelping/nettools:v1.12.0 ls /shared
10. udo -u user04 podman run -it -d  -v /data/shared:/shared --network=none --name user04 --userns=keep-id --group-add keep-groups --log-level debug quay.io/travelping/nettools:v1.12.0 ls /shared
```
### Describe the results you received

Permission denied

### Describe the results you expected

show success

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Your logs don't show any error occurring - that container started successfully. What, exactly, is going wrong?

Going purely off your reproducer and not knowing the exact error, I suspect you need `--group-add=keep-groups`. Rootless containers drop all supplemental groups by default - so anything that's not in the user's subuid/subgid, which `sharedgroup` will not be, is dropped. The `--group-add=keep-groups` argument prevents this from happening. However, I must note that the added group still not not be mapped into the user namespace, so all files in your mount will show up as being owned by group `nobody`. You'll still be able to access them but you will not be able to do things like chown to that group because it's not present in the user namespace.

Final note: `--group-add=keep-groups` is exclusive to the `crun` OCI runtime. It looks like you're using `runc` so this will not work unless you install crun. I don't believe you'll need to edit `containers.conf` as AFAIK crun is preferred over runc if both are installed.
yep, you are right, i am using runc instead of crun,  i'll try to switch to crun , thank you very much
@Luap99 Feels like we should error when using the annotation tricks that only crun supports with runc?

> [@Luap99](https://github.com/Luap99) Feels like we should error when using the annotation tricks that only crun supports with runc?

I don't think so, IMO the fact that we added the `--group-add keep-groups` short cut was a mistake as it a overloads an existing option that does something slightly different and it exposes a runtime specific option that only one runtime supports. I think returning an error is wrong because ultimately we cannot know if the runtime does support it or not and runbc could gain support for it any time (https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4642) and then we would have to patch it back out.

The current docs already say this is only supported with crun and I rather not hard code that assumption in the code.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
